case_name: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
summary: Established the 'Basic Structure Doctrine', limiting Parliament's power to amend the Constitution.

case_name: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
summary: Expanded the scope of Article 21, establishing that 'procedure established by law' must be fair, just and reasonable.

case_name: Vishaka v. State of Rajasthan
summary: Laid down the 'Vishaka Guidelines' for dealing with sexual harassment at the workplace.

case_name: Navtej Singh Johar v. Union of India
summary: Decriminalized consensual homosexual acts by reading down Section 377 of the IPC.

case_name: Justice K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India
summary: Recognized the Right to Privacy as a fundamental right under Article 21.

case_name: MC Mehta v. Union of India (Oleum Gas Leak Case)
summary: Established the principle of 'Absolute Liability' for hazardous industries.

case_name: Lalita Kumari v. Govt. of Uttar Pradesh
summary: Mandated the compulsory registration of an FIR if the information discloses a cognizable offence.

case_name: D.K. Basu v. State of West Bengal
summary: Laid down specific guidelines to be followed by police during arrest and detention to prevent custodial violence.

case_name: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India
summary: Struck down Section 66A of the IT Act as unconstitutional for violating freedom of speech.

case_name: Pawan Kumar v. State of Haryana
summary: Dealt with dowry deaths and cruelty under Section 498A and 304B of the IPC.

case_name: Salomon v A Salomon & Co Ltd
summary: Established the principle of separate legal personality of a company.

case_name: Balfour v Balfour
summary: Established that domestic agreements usually do not intend to create legal relations.
relevance: Contract Law, Intention to Create Legal Relations

case_name: Carlill v Carbolic Smoke Ball Co
summary: Established that an advertisement offering a reward is a unilateral contract.

case_name: Donoghue v Stevenson
summary: Established the modern law of negligence and the 'neighbour principle'.

case_name: TMA Pai Foundation v. State of Karnataka
summary: Clarified the rights of minority educational institutions.

case_name: Indra Sawhney v. Union of India
summary: Upheld the Mandal Commission report, setting a 50% cap on reservations.

case_name: Nirbhaya Case (Mukesh & Anr v. State for NCT of Delhi)
summary: Landmark gang rape and murder case leading to severe criminal law amendments.

case_name: Mohd. Ahmed Khan v. Shah Bano Begum
summary: Granted maintenance to a divorced Muslim woman under Section 125 of CrPC.

case_name: Daniel Latifi v. Union of India
summary: Expanded the interpretation of the Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act, 1986.

case_name: Shayara Bano v. Union of India
summary: Declared the practice of instantaneous Triple Talaq (Talaq-e-Biddat) as unconstitutional.

case_name: Mary Roy v. State of Kerala
summary: Ensured equal rights for Syrian Christian women in ancestral property.

case_name: Vineeta Sharma v. Rakesh Sharma
summary: Clarified that daughters have coparcenary rights by birth in a Hindu Undivided Family.
relevance: Hindu Succession Act, Women's Property Rights

case_name: National Legal Services Authority v. Union of India
summary: Recognized transgender people as the 'third gender' and affirmed their fundamental rights.

case_name: Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board v. A. Rajappa
summary: Expanded the definition of 'industry' under the Industrial Disputes Act.

case_name: Indian Medical Association v. V.P. Shantha
summary: Brought medical profession within the ambit of the Consumer Protection Act.

case_name: Lucknow Development Authority v. M.K. Gupta
summary: Held that statutory authorities providing services are liable under the Consumer Protection Act.

case_name: R. Rajagopal v. State of Tamil Nadu
summary: Established the right to privacy implicit in Article 21.

case_name: Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab
summary: Laid down the 'Rarest of Rare' doctrine for the imposition of capital punishment.

case_name: Hussainara Khatoon v. Home Secretary, State of Bihar
summary: Recognized the right to a speedy trial as a fundamental right under Article 21.

case_name: Selvi v. State of Karnataka
summary: Ruled that involuntary narcoanalysis, brain mapping, and polygraph tests violate Article 20(3).
